title: Localize a delivered bank (translation track)
context: A canonical item model with cleanly separated stem, choices, rationale, and locale fields is cheaper to translate than prose. Localizing a delivered bank hands it to a translation-track project (similar to the Elyos emergency-phrasebooks or braille-ready-texts pattern) and requires a language-specific SME reviewer who can verify that the translated answer keys are correct in the target language. The output license depends on the source bank's license (CC-BY-4.0 or CC-BY-SA-4.0). The target language and SME reviewer must be confirmed before this task begins.
objective: Localize one delivered question bank into a target language, with translated stems, choices, rationales, and locale metadata, reviewed by a language-specific SME.
acceptanceCriteria: A target language and language-specific SME reviewer confirmed before authoring begins.
Stems, choices, answer keys (in the target language where relevant), rationales, and locale field translated into the target language.
Translated answer keys verified correct in the target language by the language-specific SME.
Accessibility metadata (alt text) translated or confirmed language-neutral.
Locale field set correctly in all translated items.
Machine gate green (schema valid + grader self-test pass on translated items).
Human sign-offs recorded: language SME (key correctness in target language), bias reviewer (no new culturally exclusionary content introduced by translation).
resources: quiz-bank-open-model-001 (canonical item model — locale and i18n fields)
quiz-bank-open-pilot-009 or quiz-bank-open-bank-014 (source bank to translate)
quiz-bank-open-validator-006 (CI harness — run on translated items)
output: A translated question bank in canonical JSON with locale metadata, verified by a language-specific SME, schema-valid and grader-self-test-passing.
